overview: Full-stack content calendar app. Build the React/Vite frontend per this spec. All frontend files go under /Users/big_meech/gigs/calendo/src/frontend/
stack: React 18 + TypeScript 5 + Vite 5 + Tailwind CSS 3 + shadcn/ui (Radix UI) + react-day-picker v8 + date-fns v3 + react-router-dom v6
CRITICAL_alias: @/ resolves to src/frontend/ itself (the directory containing package.json). NOT a nested src/ folder. Configure in both vite.config.ts (resolve.alias) and tsconfig.json (paths). All imports like '@/components/...' and '@/lib/...' resolve relative to src/frontend/.

vite_config_ts.example: import { defineConfig } from 'vite'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; import path from 'path'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], resolve: { alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, '.') } }, server: { proxy: { '/api': { target: 'http://localhost:8000', changeOrigin: true, rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, '') } } } })
vite_config_ts.note: __dirname in vite.config.ts is src/frontend/ — so path.resolve(__dirname, '.') is the src/frontend/ directory itself

main_tsx.imports: import './globals.css'; import React from 'react'; import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'; import { BrowserRouter } from 'react-router-dom'; import App from './App';

App_tsx.description: Root router. Uses react-router-dom v6 Routes + Route.
routes: / -> if localStorage.getItem('token') exists, <Navigate to='/dashboard' /> else <Navigate to='/login' />
App_tsx.note: The root / redirect can be done with a small RootRedirect component or inline element check.

api_auth_ts.BASE_URL: All calls go through /api prefix (Vite dev proxy). In production the static site calls the API directly.

register.returns: { id: number, email: string, created_at: string }

login.body: application/x-www-form-urlencoded. Field names: username (not email!), password. This is OAuth2PasswordRequestForm format.
login.implementation: const form = new URLSearchParams(); form.append('username', email); form.append('password', password); fetch('/api/auth/login', { method: 'POST', body: form })

api_posts_ts.CRITICAL_date_handling: API sends scheduled_date as 'YYYY-MM-DD' string. To avoid timezone shift, ALWAYS parse with: new Date(dateStr + 'T00:00:00') (local time). When sending back to API, format with date-fns: format(date, 'yyyy-MM-dd').
api_posts_ts.CRITICAL_401_handler: All fetch calls must check response.status === 401. If so: clearToken() then window.location.href = '/login'. Implement in a central fetchWithAuth wrapper.

updatePost.body: Partial<{ title, caption, platform, scheduled_date, status }>

exportCsv.implementation: fetch(...) -> response.blob() -> URL.createObjectURL(blob) -> create <a> element -> click -> URL.revokeObjectURL(...)

hooks_useMediaQuery_ts.implementation: Initialize state with window.matchMedia(query).matches. useEffect: create MediaQueryList, add change listener to update state, clean up on unmount. Return boolean state.

pages_LoginPage_tsx.onSubmit: Call api/auth login(email, password). Store token+email in localStorage. navigate('/dashboard').
pages_LoginPage_tsx.error_handling: Catch API errors. Set error string to pass as error prop to AuthForm.

pages_RegisterPage_tsx.onSubmit: Call api/auth register(email, password). On success: auto-login with same credentials. Store token+email. navigate('/dashboard').

state.currentMonth: Date — use new Date() initially, then set to first day of month for display
state.modalState: { open: boolean; mode: 'create' | 'edit'; post?: PostData; scheduledDate?: Date }

data_fetching.fetchPosts: async function that calls getPosts(month='YYYY-MM'). Sets loading, sets posts, catches errors.
data_fetching.on_currentMonth_change: Call fetchPosts with new month string

post_mapping_to_calendar.description: Map ApiPost to the Post type that CalendoCalendar and CalendarMobile expect
post_mapping_to_calendar.title: apiPost.title
post_mapping_to_calendar.date: new Date(apiPost.scheduled_date + 'T00:00:00')  — CRITICAL: avoid timezone shift

platform_filter.description: Filter mapped posts by activePlatforms BEFORE passing to calendar components. CLIENT-SIDE ONLY.

handlers.onDateClick: Open modal in create mode with scheduledDate set to clicked date
handlers.onPostClick: Open modal in edit mode. Map ApiPost to PostData. Convert scheduled_date string to Date with 'T00:00:00' suffix.
handlers.onSave_create: Call createPost API. fetchPosts. Close modal.
handlers.onSave_edit: Call updatePost API. fetchPosts. Close modal.
handlers.onDelete: Call deletePost API. fetchPosts. Close modal.
handlers.onMonthChange: Update currentMonth state. fetchPosts will auto-trigger.
handlers.onTodayClick: Set currentMonth to new Date() (today). Also update any selectedDate in mobile view.
handlers.onPlatformToggle: Toggle platform in activePlatforms array. No API call.

structure: main with pt-14 (to clear fixed navbar)
CalendarMobile with filteredPosts (takes full remaining height below navbar)
layout_mobile.note: CalendarMobile internally has the floating + button

pages_DashboardPage_tsx.responsive_swap: const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)'). Render CalendarMobile if isMobile, else CalendoCalendar + PlatformFilter.
pages_DashboardPage_tsx.empty_and_loading: If loading: show LoadingSkeleton. If !loading && posts.length === 0: show EmptyState. Otherwise show calendar.

components_ProtectedRoute_tsx.logic: const token = localStorage.getItem('token'); if (!token) return <Navigate to='/login' replace />; return <>{children}</>;

implementation: Format currentMonth as 'yyyy-MM' using date-fns format()
On click: fetch('/api/posts/export/csv?month=' + monthStr, { headers: getAuthHeader() })
components_ExportButton_tsx.button_style: Use Button component, variant='outline', style matching calendo dark theme

components_ErrorToast_tsx.behavior: Fixed position (bottom-center or top-right). Auto-dismiss after 4 seconds using useEffect. Show only when message is non-null.
components_ErrorToast_tsx.style: Dark surface background, accent-colored border or icon

copied_components.ONLY_MODIFICATION: Remove the 'use client' directive (line 1) from each file. That is the ONLY change. Do not refactor or modify the component logic.

shadcn_ui_components.note: Implement standard shadcn/ui components. These all use Radix UI primitives and class-variance-authority. Import cn from '@/lib/utils'.

button_tsx.variants: default (accent bg), outline (border only), ghost (no border), destructive

shadcn_ui_components.input_tsx: Styled <input> with dark theme: bg-[#0F0F0F] border-[#2A2A2A] text-[#F5F5F5] focus-visible:ring-[#E1306C]/50
shadcn_ui_components.card_tsx: Card, CardHeader, CardContent, CardFooter, CardTitle, CardDescription. Dark surface styling.
shadcn_ui_components.badge_tsx: Badge with default and outline variants. Dark themed.
shadcn_ui_components.dialog_tsx: Uses @radix-ui/react-dialog. Sub-components: Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogFooter. Dark overlay and content background (#1A1A1A).
shadcn_ui_components.select_tsx: Uses @radix-ui/react-select. Sub-components: Select, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SelectContent, SelectItem. Dark themed dropdown.
shadcn_ui_components.popover_tsx: Uses @radix-ui/react-popover. Sub-components: Popover, PopoverTrigger, PopoverContent. Dark background.

calendar_tsx.CRITICAL: This is react-day-picker v8. Import: import { DayPicker } from 'react-day-picker'. Props: mode='single', selected, onSelect, initialFocus. v9 has breaking API changes.
calendar_tsx.dark_styling: Selected day gets #E1306C background. Today gets ring. Hover states use #2A2A2A.
calendar_tsx.wrapper: export function Calendar({ className, classNames, showOutsideDays = true, ...props }: CalendarProps) { return <DayPicker showOutsideDays={showOutsideDays} ... /> }

spinner_tsx.description: Custom component — NOT in shadcn. Simple loading spinner.
spinner_tsx.implementation: SVG circle with animate-spin Tailwind class, or CSS border-based spinner. Export as Spinner with optional className prop.
spinner_tsx.example: <svg className={cn('animate-spin h-4 w-4', className)} xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' viewBox='0 0 24 24'><circle className='opacity-25' cx='12' cy='12' r='10' stroke='currentColor' strokeWidth='4'/><path className='opacity-75' fill='currentColor' d='M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4z'/></svg>

when_opening_edit_modal.description: In DashboardPage onPostClick handler, build PostData for the modal
when_opening_edit_modal.scheduledDate: new Date(apiPost.scheduled_date + 'T00:00:00')  — CRITICAL for correct date

PostModal_date_integration.when_saving: PostModal.onSave provides PostData with scheduledDate as Date. Convert back: format(postData.scheduledDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd') for API call.

critical_pitfalls_checklist: 1. @/ alias MUST resolve to src/frontend/ (not a nested src/ inside it). Wrong alias = broken imports everywhere.
2. Remove 'use client' from ALL 8 copied components. It's a Next.js directive — it breaks Vite builds.
3. Date parsing: ALWAYS new Date(dateStr + 'T00:00:00'). Without the time suffix, Date constructor treats YYYY-MM-DD as UTC midnight, which shifts to previous day in negative UTC offset timezones.
4. Login API uses application/x-www-form-urlencoded with field name 'username' (OAuth2 spec), not JSON with 'email'.
5. Vite proxy: /api requests are stripped of the /api prefix before forwarding to localhost:8000. So /api/posts becomes /posts on the backend.
6. On 401 from any API call: clear token + email from localStorage, redirect to /login.
7. react-day-picker v8 — NOT v9. Package is react-day-picker@^8.10.1. The DayPicker API changed significantly in v9.
8. Platform filter is FRONTEND-ONLY. Filter the posts array in memory. Do NOT pass platform to GET /posts API.
9. CalendoCalendar expects post.id as string. Map from API's numeric id with String(id).
10. DashboardPage main content needs pt-14 (padding-top: 3.5rem) to clear the fixed h-14 navbar.
11. globals.css must define calendo-* classes in @layer utilities. These are used by CalendarGrid and other pre-built components.
12. FeedbackButton.onSubmit: in beta, can just open an external URL or console.log. The modal is built — just wire the submit.
